data.title: Coinbase CEO aims for 50% of code from AI by October amid rising interest in AI-assisted coding.
data.description: The CEO of a cryptocurrency exchange has announced a goal for 50% of their daily code to be generated by artificial intelligence, reflecting the increasing popularity of AI-assisted programming. This shift highlights a significant trend in the industry as companies look to utilize AI technologies for coding purposes.

data.title: SAIC reports second quarter FY2026 results, with revenue totaling $2.5 billion, showing growth in defense and intelligence sectors.
data.description: In Reston, Virginia on September 4, 2025, Science Applications International Corporation (NASDAQ: SAIC) disclosed its second-quarter outcomes for the period ending on August 1, 2025. SAIC, a top Fortune 500 technology company, specializes in advancing the nation's digital evolution within defense, space, civilian, and intelligence sectors.

data.description: The price of EIGEN is currently trading at $1.21 on the Binance spot market. The Relative Strength Index (RSI) stands at 46.26, indicating a neutral position. Despite this, there is a weak bullish trend observed as the price remains below important moving averages.

data.title: Japan’s regulator suggests updating rules for cryptocurrencies to align with securities law, transferring oversight from the Payment Services Act to the Financial Instruments and Exchange Act.
data.description: The Financial Services Agency in Japan has suggested transferring the regulation of cryptocurrency from the Payment Services Act to the more rigorous Financial Instruments and Exchange Act. This move aims to enhance oversight and control over crypto-related activities in the country. The proposed shift reflects a strategic effort to bolster regulations in the cryptocurrency sector by subjecting it to stricter rules. This change would significantly impact the oversight of cryptocurrencies in Japan, potentially leading to more stringent monitoring and enforcement.

data.title: Thomson Reuters sets up $1.0 billion share repurchase plan and NCIB to buy up to 10 million shares.
data.description: Today, Thomson Reuters (TSX/Nasdaq: TRI) revealed their plan for a $1.0 billion share repurchase program and normal course issuer bid (NCIB) to acquire up to 10 million shares. This initiative comes as part of the company's efforts to enhance shareholder value and optimize their capital structure. Thomson Reuters, a prominent global content and technology firm, is taking strategic steps to solidify its position in the market.

data.title: Waterdrop Inc., a tech platform focusing on insurance and healthcare, reported net profit of RMB140.2 million in Q2 2025, a 58.7% YoY increase. Insurance premiums rose sharply at RMB3,204.3 million, operating revenue increased to RMB838.0 million. They launched AI applications improving user experience and revenue, reported 999 patient enrollments, and issued US$10.9 million cash dividend. Cash position as of June 30, 2025, was RMB3,406.7 million. Additionally, they have repurchased 55.7 million ADSs for US$109.0 million, and initiated a new share repurchase program. Shareholders to receive a cash dividend of approximately US$10.9 million.
data.description: Waterdrop Inc. reported its financial results for the second quarter of 2025, with a net profit of RMB 140.2 million (US$19.6 million), up 58.7% YoY. Insurance premiums reached RMB 3,204.3 million (US$447.3 million), a 80.2% increase YoY. Medical crowdfunding saw 480 million people donate RMB 70.0 billion to 3.54 million patients. The company enrolled 12,216 patients into clinical trials. Operating revenue rose by 23.9% to RMB 838.0 million (US$117.0 million). Operating costs increased by 18.7%, sales and marketing expenses rose by 26.3%, and general and administrative expenses decreased by 21.9%. Operating profit was RMB 97.3 million (US$13.6 million). The cash position stood at RMB 3,406.7 million (US$475.6 million). The company has repurchased 55.7 million ADSs for US$109.0 million and approved a new share repurchase program up to US$50 million. A cash dividend of US$0.03 per ADS or US$0.003 per ordinary share, totaling around US$10.9 million, was approved. The company aims for top and bottom-line growth in the second half of 2025 with a focus on tech-driven innovation.

data.title: Recall.ai secures $38M in Series B funding, reaching $250M valuation; expanding conversation data solutions.
data.description: Recall.ai, a San Francisco-based company known for its API for meeting recordings, has recently secured $38 million in Series B funding. This funding round has valued the company at $250 million. Bessemer Venture Partners led the funding, with investments also coming in from HubSpot Ventures and Salesforce Ventures. The company plans to use this capital injection to diversify its offerings, moving beyond meeting integrations and introducing new features such as the Desktop Recording SDK, dialers, phone, and in-person conversation data capture capabilities.

data.title: Agentic automation highlighted at ABBYY Ascend Fall event in Austin on October 14, 2025.
data.description: In Austin, Texas, ABBYY Ascend Fall on October 14, 2025, is set to showcase the latest advancements in agentic automation. This event will highlight the future of automation, with a focus on empowering businesses through innovative technology.

data.title: "EmPOWER AI 2025 conference in Napa unites 40 global companies to discuss AI's impact on energy sector."
data.description: A conference called EmPOWER AI presented by Bidgely is scheduled to take place from September 16-18 in Napa, California. Over 40 global companies will be represented at this event, which serves as a platform for dialogue among utility leaders, data experts, and AI executives regarding the transformative impact of AI in the energy sector. The conference aims to showcase the latest AI advancements, building on the success of last year's debut where Bidgely introduced groundbreaking innovations.
